title: Semantic lint rule inventory, false-positive policy, and engine selection
description: Source-owned L1 inventory for Asupersync-specific semantic lint rules before implementation. The artifact records rule risk, preferred detection substrate, false-positive posture, allow-marker shape, owner bead, and required tests.

rule_rows.title: Await while holding obligation, permit, lease, or lock guard

engine_decision.rationale: This rule needs type-aware guard lifetime analysis across await suspension points. Text matching is too brittle because guard names, aliases, tuple fields, and helper-returned guards vary across sync, channel, obligation, registry, and database code.

rejected_engines.reason: Can catch obvious local bindings but cannot prove guard type or drop-before-await under aliases.
Would duplicate compiler lifetime/type information and invite false positives.

source_patterns: .await with a live guard implementing Drop
NameLease or permit held across .await
Mutex/RwLock/ContendedMutex guard held across .await

expected_false_positives: guards intentionally dropped by helper calls before await
zero-sized proof tokens without resource ownership

tests_required: positive fixture: lock guard held across await is rejected
positive fixture: NameLease held across await is rejected
negative fixture: explicit drop before await is accepted
allow-marker fixture requires reason and owner

rule_rows.title: Long-running loop without Cx checkpoint or bounded yield

engine_decision.rationale: AST discovery can find candidate loops, but the decision needs HIR/control-flow awareness to recognize checkpoint wrappers, bounded iteration, and loop exits without forcing noisy comments into every tight loop.

rejected_engines.reason: Good candidate finder, insufficient to prove bounded loops or checkpoint wrapper calls.
Feature graph data does not expose control flow.

source_patterns: loop/while in async fn without cx.checkpoint, yield_now, budget check, or explicit bounded counter
manual polling loop without cancellation checkpoint
retry loop without budget meet or cancellation observation

expected_false_positives: bounded loops where the bound is established by a helper
pure CPU loops inside explicitly documented blocking/offloaded contexts

tests_required: positive fixture: infinite async loop without checkpoint is rejected
negative fixture: loop with cx.checkpoint is accepted
negative fixture: statically bounded loop is accepted
structured diagnostic names the missing checkpoint

rule_rows.title: Ambient wall-clock or randomness in lab-sensitive modules

engine_decision.rationale: The risky call sites are syntactically stable and should be scarce in lab/replay-sensitive modules. A lightweight AST matcher can flag std time, system entropy, thread rng, and wall_now usage with clear path-scoped allow rules.

rejected_engines.reason: Type-aware analysis is useful later but not needed for the first high-signal path-scoped ban.
AST matching is less brittle than line-oriented token scanning.

source_patterns: std::time::SystemTime::now
std::time::Instant::now
crate::time::wall_now in deterministic lab paths
rand::thread_rng or OS entropy in replay-sensitive paths

expected_false_positives: operator diagnostics that intentionally record host wall time outside deterministic replay
test-only comparisons that assert virtual-time normalization

tests_required: positive fixture: SystemTime::now in src/lab is rejected
negative fixture: VirtualClock or lab now is accepted
allow fixture must include owner bead
golden diagnostic names deterministic replay risk

rule_rows.title: Outcome severity ignored or collapsed

engine_decision.rationale: Outcome is a semantic lattice. The lint must distinguish safe transformations from code that maps Panicked or Cancelled to Ok/Err without preserving severity. That requires type-aware matching and exhaustiveness context.

rejected_engines.reason: Can spot suspicious matches but cannot reliably prove the matched type is Outcome or that severity was preserved through helpers.

source_patterns: match Outcome where Panicked or Cancelled is mapped to Ok/Err
Outcome::severity result computed and ignored
conversion from Outcome<T> to Result<T, _> without severity note

expected_false_positives: test harness code intentionally constructing reduced examples
display-only summaries that preserve full outcome elsewhere

tests_required: positive fixture: Cancelled collapsed to Ok is rejected
negative fixture: severity-preserving conversion is accepted
positive fixture: ignored severity call is rejected
diagnostic suggests preserving the Outcome lattice

rule_rows.title: Race loser handled by drop rather than explicit cancel and drain

engine_decision.rationale: The rule needs AST candidate discovery for select/race/drop patterns plus type-aware confirmation that the loser owns a TaskHandle, permit, or future with cancellation obligations.

rejected_engines.reason: Insufficient for handle/future ownership and defuse-drop-abort semantics.
Feature graph checks cannot reason about race loser lifecycle.

source_patterns: select/race branch drops the losing handle without abort or join
drop-based cancellation without explicit CancelReason
defuse_drop_abort without a paired loser-drain proof

expected_false_positives: local futures that do not own runtime tasks
drop guards whose Drop implementation performs explicit cancel and drain

tests_required: positive fixture: loser handle is dropped without abort/join
negative fixture: loser aborts and joins with race_loser reason
negative fixture: documented drop guard proves explicit drain
diagnostic names loser-drain invariant

rule_rows.title: Cleanup, drain, or finalizer work without bounded budget

engine_decision.rationale: The first pass can flag direct calls to cleanup/drain/finalizer APIs in code regions that do not pass Budget, shutdown_budget, deadline, or meet-derived values. Later HIR can reduce false positives.

rejected_engines.reason: No feature graph property captures cleanup budget use.

source_patterns: cleanup/drain/finalizer call without Budget argument
Duration::from_secs used as ad hoc cleanup cap outside config
shutdown path that uses Budget::INFINITE without documented owner

expected_false_positives: pure synchronous cleanup that cannot block
test fixtures that intentionally use Budget::INFINITE

tests_required: positive fixture: async cleanup with Budget::INFINITE is reported
negative fixture: cleanup budget derived by meet is accepted
inventory report includes path and call expression
allow marker requires reason and owner

rule_rows.title: Tokio dependency path leaks into core production feature graph

engine_decision.rationale: This is a dependency-graph invariant, not a source syntax invariant. Cargo metadata and cargo tree inversion are the canonical substrate, with documented carve-outs for compat, conformance, dev/test, and fuzz-only generated messages.

rejected_engines.reason: Source token checks miss transitive dependency leakage.
HIR sees compiled crate items, not the complete feature/dependency graph policy.

source_patterns: normal-edge tokio path under default asupersync production graph
normal-edge tokio path under metrics feature graph
undocumented workspace/fuzz/test tokio path
tests_required: positive fixture: default normal-edge tokio path is rejected
negative fixture: compat crate carve-out is accepted with explicit scope
negative fixture: fuzz-only generated-message edge is quarantined
proof command uses RCH and cargo tree inversion

implementation_order.reason: Existing no-tokio policy is explicit and graph-based, so implementation can be deterministic with low false-positive risk.
High value for replay determinism and a syntactically stable first matcher.
Highest correctness risk, but needs type-aware design before deny mode.

no_claims: This artifact does not implement any lint rule.
This artifact does not certify that the current source tree is free of semantic lint violations.
This artifact does not replace compiler checks, clippy, lab oracles, RCH proof lanes, or manual review.
This artifact does not authorize local Cargo fallback for future lint proof lanes.
proof_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_semantic_lint_rule_inventory CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test semantic_lint_rule_inventory_contract -- --nocapture
